pundit.name: Landon Donovan
pundit.take: **Look, I get why people might think Gunner's Canada pick is about as inspired as a lukewarm cup of coffee, but at least it's better than Devin's Bosnia, bless his heart.** You'd think with Canada hosting games in Toronto, "Bubba G" might have seen a spark of potential, but it seems even home advantage can't save a team when the manager's more focused on finding obscure snacks than in actual predictive structure. Meanwhile, I feel for Devin, I really do—he means well, but when "Ghost Pepper" decided Bosnia was his Tier 3 darling, he must have been daydreaming about 1994 instead of 2026. I mean, these two managers are hoping for some kind of miracle clash that won't actually change their zero-point fortunes. I'm calling this one as a dull draw that even the most optimistic fan wouldn't bother staying up for.

pundit_takes.headline: Gayden Rockets to Top as England Secures Iconic First Win
pundit_takes.subtitle: Unfamiliar territory for the Backpass Assassin, accustomed to last place finishes.

pundit_takes.headline: Gunner's Tactics Under Fire as Canada Faces Bosnia
pundit_takes.subtitle: Bubba G bewildered as neither side scores the upset advantage.

pundit_takes.headline: Zach's Brazil Pick Falls Flat in Last Place Disaster
pundit_takes.subtitle: Mustard Boy's condiment-first strategy leaves him with zero points.

rome_analytics_quotes: Gunner, favorite to win, slipped 3 ranks. Teenager Rafe's antics hit him hard!
Rafe's non-qualifiers draft blunder is comedy gold, yet he jumps to second place.
Gayden crushed Zach while training his son for dinner-table domination. Awkward holiday coming up!
Sim still doesn't get it: chaos agent Rafe making noise despite no soccer knowledge.
